data.title: EQT: Proposal is Subject to Approval at EQT's Annual Shareholders' Meeting on 27 May
EQT Proposes Jacob Wallenberg Jr as New Board Member
Shinih Enterprise Board OKs NT$27 Million Land Acquisition
data.description:
Shinih Enterprise's board approved the purchase of land in Tainan, Taiwan from an affiliated party, Hsieh Ya-Nan, for NT$26.9 million.

The land spanning 3,552 square meters, will be used to support the fabric manufacturer's plant expansion needs, according to a Friday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: Swedish Monthly GDP Shrinks in January
data.description:
The Swedish economy shrank by 0.5% month-over-month in January 2025, following a downwardly revised 0.5% expansion in December.

This marks the first contraction in three periods and the sharpest since November 2023, mainly due to weaker household consumption as well as lower production in manufacturing and construction.

On a yearly basis, the Swedish GDP expanded 2%, moderating from a 3.4% growth in the previous month.

data.title: Swedish New Orders Soar in January
data.description:
Total orders received by Swedish industry surged by 16.7% year-on-year in January 2025, accelerating from a 5.8% gain in the previous month and marking the third consecutive month of increase.

Orders from the foreign market increased sharply (30.2% vs 17.0% in December), while domestic demand fell at a slower pace (-2.3% vs -11.5%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, new orders grew by 2.0%, slowing slightly from an upwardly revised 2.2% rise in December, representing the softest gain since last October.

In 2024, total orders received by Swedish industry shrank by 2.9% compared to the previous year, due to a 1.3% drop in the foreign market and a 5.5% decline in the domestic market.

data.title: China Stocks Fall on Weak Inflation Data
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite slipped 0.19% to close at 3,366, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 0.17% to 10,826 on Monday, extending losses from the previous session as persistent deflationary pressures highlighted China’s economic challenges.

Weekend data showed that consumer prices fell more than expected in February, hitting a 13-month low, while producer prices declined for the 29th consecutive month, though at the slowest pace since August 2024.

Adding to the pressure, China announced fresh retaliatory tariffs amid the escalating global trade war, including a 100% tariff on certain Canadian agricultural imports and a 25% levy on aquatic products and pork.

Notable losses included Talkweb Information (-6.4%), Contemporary Amperex (-1.6%), Zhejiang Huatie (-10%), Zhejiang Dahua (-2.4%), and Hand Enterprise (-8.3%).

data.title: Mueller Water Products Insider Sold Shares Worth $4,864,588, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Steven S Heinrichs, Former Chief Financial Officer and Chief Legal and Compliance Officer, on March 05, 2025, sold 195,433 shares in Mueller Water Products for $4,864,588. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Heinrichs has control over a total of 11,399 common shares of the company, with 11,399 shares held directly.

SEC Filing:

www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1350593/000122520825003172/xslF345X05/doc4.xml

data.title: Chasen Holdings Repurchases Shares
data.description:
Chasen Holdings bought back 250,900 shares on Friday for SG$17,986, according to a same-day filing on the Singapore Stock Exchange.

The company has repurchased 3.9 million shares under its current mandate, which allows it to repurchase up to 38.7 million shares.

data.title: GlobalData Posts Lower FY24 Attributable Profit; Revenue Rises
data.description:
GlobalData said Monday its profit for the full year 2024 decreased, while revenue grew year over year.

Profit attributable to equity holders of the parent for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 29.6 million pounds sterling, compared with 30.8 million pounds earlier. EPS moved to 0.037 pound from 0.038 pound.

On the other hand, adjusted profit was 58.8 million pounds and adjusted EPS was 0.074 pound, compared with the year-ago period's 55.1 million pounds and 0.067 pound, respectively.

The data, analytics, insights, and technology company's revenue was 285.5 million pounds, compared with 273.1 million pounds a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a final dividend of 0.01 pound per share for the second half, payable May 2 to shareholders on record March 21. It paid 0.032 pound per share a year ago. This takes the full-year dividend to 0.025 pound per share, down from 0.046 pound in the previous year.

The company affirmed its target for an annualized revenue of 500 million pounds by the end of 2026.

data.title: South Korea's Kospi Rises 0.3%, Led by Oil-Refinery, Battery Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: South Korea's benchmark Kospi rose 0.3% to close at 2570.39, led by oil-refinery and battery stocks. Wall Street's gains Friday supported sentiment, with retail and institutional investors being net buyers. Energy stocks advanced. Oil refiners SK Innovation and S-Oil rose 6.0% and 8.7%, respectively. Electric-vehicle battery maker LG Energy Solution gained 3.1%. Meanwhile, Korea Zinc jumped 14% as its leading rival shareholders were set to renew their battle for control of the smelter following a local court ruling. USD/KRW settled 0.4% higher at 1,452.30 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield was up 0.8 basis points at 2.781%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: JX Advanced Metals Sets IPO Price at Y820 Apiece
data.description:
(06:39 GMT) *JX Advanced Metals: to Offer Shares at Y820 Apiece
(06:42 GMT) *JX Advanced Metals Previously Expected to Offer Shares around Y815 Apiece
(07:25 GMT) JX Advanced Metals Sets IPO Price at Y820 Apiece

By Kosaku Narioka

Eneos Holdings' metals business plans to offer shares at 820 yen, equivalent to $5.54, apiece in its initial public offering.

This compares with the previously projected offering price of approximately Y815.

Eneos announced in February that it planned to spin off its metals subsidiary and raise billions of dollars, partly to fund investments needed to expand the supply of next-generation energy sources, such as synthetic fuels and hydrogen.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: Zig Sheng Industrial Decides Against Paying Dividend for 2024
data.description:
Zig Sheng Industrial will not distribute dividends for 2024 following a board resolution on Friday, according to a same-day Taiwan Exchange filing.

The polyester yarns manufacturer did not disclose further details regarding the decision.

data.title: SIA Engineering Buys Back More Shares
data.description:
SIA Engineering repurchased 38,200 shares in the open market on Friday for SG$90,547, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The engineering company has been authorized to buy back 22.5 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has bought back 5.7 million shares.

data.title: Five Years After GameStop Chaos, Robinhood Again Sanctioned by FINRA for $29.75 Million
data.description:
Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA) has ordered Robinhood (NASDAQ: HOOD) to pay $29.75 million for multiple regulatory violations, including anti-money laundering (AML) failures and inadequate supervision of its trading systems during critical market events, namely meme-stock frenzy from the COVID-19 era.

Robinhood Hit With $29.75 Million FINRA Penalty for Compliance Failures

The settlement announced last week requires Robinhood Financial to pay $3.75 million in restitution to customers affected by its order "collaring" practices, while both Robinhood Financial and Robinhood Securities will pay a combined $26 million fine for widespread compliance deficiencies.

"Today's action reminds FINRA members that compliance with core regulatory obligations remains critical to safeguarding and serving all investors," said Bill St. Louis, Executive Vice President and Head of Enforcement at FINRA, noting that innovative financial services must still adhere to fundamental regulatory requirements.

It is worth noting that this is the second fine imposed by FINRA on Robinhood in the past four years related to the app's actions in March 2020. During that time, trading outages occurred due to speculation on meme stocks such as GameStop, leaving retail investors unable to trade.

August Iorio
@august_iorio

Late on Friday evening (timing not by accident), FINRA announced an agreement with Robinhood to end numerous regulatory probes for nearly $30 million.

This is the second such agreement in less than 4 years. Back in 2021, Robinhood paid nearly $70 million to end various…

Mar 09, 2025

„FINRA had the opportunity to do the right thing and hold Robinhood accountable for all of its negligence that led to the trading restrictions that harmed so many people, but it took the easy way out, only holding Robinhood accountable for its clearing technology failures from that period,” commented on X (former Twitter) August Iorio, the securities arbitration attorney.

Moreover, the penalty and settlement come at a time when another American institution, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), has closed a year-long investigation into the company's activities, signaling a potentially significant shift in the country's regulatory approach.

Multiple Violations Across AML and Trading Systems

FINRA's investigation uncovered multiple serious violations across Robinhood's operations. The firms failed to establish adequate anti-money laundering programs, missing red flags related to manipulative trading, suspicious money movements, and account takeovers by hackers. Robinhood Financial also opened thousands of accounts without properly verifying customer identities.

During the meme stock trading frenzy of early 2021, Robinhood Securities failed to adequately supervise its clearing technology system despite warning signs of processing delays. The system ultimately experienced severe latency in January 2021 as trading volume surged, hampering the firm's ability to meet regulatory obligations.

"The clearing system experienced severe latency due to a surge in trading volume and volatility, which impacted Robinhood's clearing operations," FINRA stated in its findings.

The regulator also found that Robinhood Financial misled customers about its practice of "collaring" market orders by converting them to limit orders. This resulted in some orders being canceled, forcing customers to resubmit trades that ultimately executed at worse prices. The $3.75 million restitution will compensate these affected customers.

Additionally, the online broker failed to properly supervise paid social media influencers who promoted the platform with statements FINRA deemed "promissory or not fair and balanced, and thus misleading to investors."

Pattern of Regulatory Issues Amid Financial Success

The settlement comes just two months after Robinhood paid $45 million to the SEC for violations of securities laws, including failure to preserve electronic customer communications between 2020 and 2021.

Both Robinhood entities consented to FINRA's findings without admitting or denying the charges and agreed to certify that they have remediated the issues identified in the settlement.

The regulatory action comes despite Robinhood's record financial performance in late 2024, when it reported $916 million in net income on over $1 billion in revenue, with crypto trading becoming an increasingly significant part of its business.

data.title: Norway Producer Inflation Surges to 23%
data.description:
The annual producer inflation in Norway jumped to 23.3% in February 2025, the highest since September 2022, from 18.1% in the previous month.

Prices rose sharply across all sub-indexes, namely oil and natural gas extraction (43% vs. 37.5% in December), electricity, gas, and steam (40.3% vs. -8.4%), energy goods (40.2% vs. 29.1%), and manufacturing (6.5% vs. 5.6%).

Excluding energy goods, producer prices increased 5.8% year-on-year in February, easing from a 6.2% rise in January.

On a monthly basis, the PPI grew 1.7%, slowing from a 4.7% jump in the prior period.

data.title: Admiral, Deutsche Lufthansa, Galderma Group
data.description:
Securities analysts revised their ratings and price targets on several European companies, including Admiral, Deutsche Lufthansa and Galderma Group on Monday.

HIGHLIGHTS

  • * Admiral : RBC raises target price to 3800p from 3550p

  • * Deutsche Lufthansa AG : JP Morgan raises target price to EUR 5.5 from EUR 5

  • * Galderma Group AG : RBC cuts to sector perform from outperform

Following is a summary of research actions on European companies reported by Reuters on Monday. Stock entries are in alphabetical order.

  • * Admiral : RBC raises target price to 3800p from 3550p

  • * Deutsche Lufthansa AG : JP Morgan raises target price to EUR 5.5 from EUR 5

  • * Galderma Group AG : RBC cuts to sector perform from outperform

  • * Galderma Group AG : RBC raises target price to CHF 106 from CHF 101

  • * IAG : RBC raises target price to 440p from 350p

  • * Quilter Plc : JP Morgan cuts target price to 172p from 177p

  • * SIG Plc : RBC cuts target price to 16p from 22p

data.title: Seatrium's Valuation Looks Attractive to Citi — Market Talk
data.description: Seatrium's valuation and its share buyback program, which could help limit further volatility risk, drive Citi Research to upgrade the stock to buy from neutral while maintaining its target price of S$2.65. After the integrated shipyard's recent share-price correction, the stock is now trading at attractive levels, analyst Luis Hilado says in a note. A significant portion of Seatrium's approved S$100 million share buyback program remains in place, he adds. There is a possibility that the company may conduct further buybacks and its share purchase mandate could be renewed again at the upcoming annual general meeting, he says. Shares are last at S$2.14. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0717 GMT - Seatrium's valuation and its share buyback program, which could help limit further volatility risk, drive Citi Research to upgrade the stock to buy from neutral while maintaining its target price of S$2.65. After the integrated shipyard's recent share-price correction, the stock is now trading at attractive levels, analyst Luis Hilado says in a note. A significant portion of Seatrium's approved S$100 million share buyback program remains in place, he adds. There is a possibility that the company may conduct further buybacks and its share purchase mandate could be renewed again at the upcoming annual general meeting, he says. Shares are last at S$2.14. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0655 GMT - European military budgets will grow at least at a high-single-digit until 2035, in parallel with a push by regional governments to order European defense equipment over U.S. kit, boosting European defense stocks, Berenberg analysts say. They note that Britain's commitment to raise U.K. defense spending to 2.5% of its gross domestic product by 2027 was higher than they anticipated, and will benefit QinetiQ and Babcock International in particular, which get more than half of their revenue from the U.K. Ministry of Defence. Berenberg upgraded Babcock International to buy from hold, saying consensus growth estimates are too low, and downgraded BAE Systems and Chemring to hold from buy due to defense budget uncertainty in the U.S., which provides the highest share of their revenue. Its top pick is German arms maker Rheinmetall. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0647 GMT - Chinese equity markets have better priced in the AI theme now, Goldman Sachs analysts say in a research note. The valuation premium between U.S. and China tech proxies has narrowed from 152% at the beginning of the year to 71% now after the emergence of DeepSeek, they note. "Profitability improvements and earnings upgrades are probably needed to drive further gains in the AI-Tech universe," they say. Meanwhile, Chinese policymakers' clear signals of a pro-growth policy stance and low tolerance for systemic risk during the NPC meeting are critical for "anchoring growth expectations and keeping policy risk premium in check," GS adds. However, GS cautions that U.S.-China trade tensions could be a major risk factor in the coming months. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0639 GMT - True Corp.'s earnings may benefit from easing competition and tax loss carry-forward, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Wasu Mattanapotchanart says in a research report. Competition in Thailand abated in February, with both True and rival Advanced Info Service lowering data quotas, the analyst notes. These data-quota reductions could result in a 3%-7% increase in prepaid average revenue per user over the next two quarters, based on the industry's track record. True's chief financial officer also expects the effective tax rate to be substantially below 20% over the next few years as the telecom operator plans to use tax loss carry-forward. The brokerage has a buy rating and target price of THB14.20 on the shares, which are 0.9% lower at THB10.90. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0623 GMT - JD.com's earnings are set to benefit further from the government's expanded consumer goods trade-in program, Nomura analysts write in a note. While sales of home appliances have slowed due to front-loaded demand, laptop sales have sustained their momentum, they say. Meanwhile, sales of smartphones could get a boost after the government increased the number of categories eligible for subsidies under the program, the analysts say. Nomura raises its 2025 revenue forecast by 4%, as it now expects JD.com's retail segment to post a 8.3% increase in revenue. The Chinese online retailer's guidance was also higher than expected, it notes. The brokerage maintains a buy rating and raises the target price on ADRs to $55.00 from $50.00. JD.com last closed at $42.29. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0615 GMT - General merchandise will be a major growth driver this and next year for JD.com, Deutsche Bank's Jessie Xu writes in a note. Electronics, home appliances and general merchandise achieved double-digit 4Q revenue growth. There is some upside potential for JD in optimizing product mix, lowering procurement costs and generating operating efficiency gains, Xu adds. The fashion category is also gaining momentum due to better product offerings and persistent efforts to gain consumer mindshare, Xu says. Deutsche maintains a buy rating on JD and raises its target price to HK$193.00 from HK$190.00. Shares are last at HK$162.20. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0607 GMT - Sappe's business recovery in 2025 faces some headwinds, Thanachart Securities' Pattadol Bunnak says in a research report, as the brokerage downgrades the stock to hold from buy and cuts its target price to THB41.00 from THB87.00. Some issues in four export markets comprising 35% of the beverage producer's total sales have emerged, the analyst says. The company expects no orders from its U.K. distributor in 1H partly owing to ongoing inventory destocking, while it has problems with its Indonesian distributor, and its competition in South Korea is re-emerging, the analyst notes. The brokerage cuts its earnings estimates for Sappe by 19% for 2025, 28% for 2026, and 35% for 2027. Shares are 3.3% lower at THB37.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0506 GMT - Q&M Dental Group (Singapore) may benefit from its more aggressive clinic expansion plan, CGS International's Tay Wee Kuang says in a research report. At its 2024 results' announcement, the dental healthcare group highlighted expansion plans in Singapore and across Southeast Asia and China, the analyst notes. Its clinics in Malaysia could witness a mix of organic and inorganic expansion after it flagged possible opportunities in Johor state following recent commitments by Malaysia and Singapore to develop the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone. The brokerage lifts its 2025-2026 EPS forecasts for the company by 3.9%-7.2%. It raises the target price on the stock to S$0.43 from S$0.37, with an unchanged add rating. Shares are 4.2% higher at S$0.30. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Norway Inflation Rate Rises to 10-Month High
data.description:
The annual consumer inflation rate in Norway rose to 3.6% in February 2025 from 2.3% in the previous month.

This marked the highest reading since April 2024, as prices accelerated for food and non-alcoholic beverages (7.5% vs 5% in January), housing and utilities (5.1% vs 2%), and transport (1.7% vs 1.3%).

In addition, costs increased at a faster pace for furnishings, household equipment and routine maintenance (1.8% vs 1.1%), communications (5.2% vs 2.3%), and restaurants and hotels (3.9% vs 1.9%).

On the other hand, prices moderated for alcoholic beverages and tobacco (4% vs 4.7%), health (5% vs 5.2%), and recreation and culture (2.8% vs 3%).

On a monthly basis, the headline CPI climbed 1.4% from 0.2% in January.

Meanwhile, the CPI adjusted for tax changes and excluding energy products (CPI-ATE) increased by 3.4% year-on-year and 1% month-on-month, compared to 2.8% and 0.1%, respectively, in the previous month.

data.title: German Industrial Production Rose at Start of 2025 as Tariff Threats Loom
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

Industrial production in Germany showed tentative signs of improvement at the start of the year, though that could be threatened by potential U.S. trade tariffs.

Output increased 2.0% on month in January, German statistics agency Destatis said Monday, compared with a 1.5% decline in the final month of 2024. Economists polled by The Wall Street Journal expected a 1.5% increase in January.

There are signs the industrial recession over recent years is bottoming out. The less volatile three-month on three-month comparison showed that the production from November to January was stable compared with the prior three months.

January's output rise was driven by the automotive industry, where production jumped 6.4%. Production in the energy-intensive industry, which has recently struggled under high energy costs, also increased.

However, German industry is bracing for U.S. import tariffs threatened by the Trump administration. Germany is particularly vulnerable as a major exporter to the U.S.

Indeed, the production data comes after industrial orders at German factories--a signal of production to come--fell back sharply at the start of the year.

But fiscal stimulus announced by Germany's likely next chancellor, Friedrich Merz, last week could bring an upswing, spurred especially by a boost in defense spending.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Artisan Slams Seven & i Over Leadership Choi, Urges Action on Couche-Tard Bid
data.description:
Artisan Partners urged Seven & i Holdings to address conflicts of interest in its leadership and engage with Alimentation Couche-Tard's (ACT) takeover offer, according to a Sunday company statement.

Artisan, which holds a stake in the Japanese firm, criticized Stephen Dacus for leading the special committee while being part of the nomination process that named him CEO. It argued Dacus should have stepped down from both roles.

Artisan slammed the board for rejecting ACT's $14.86 per share offer, noting Seven & i's stock has since fallen to $14.18, below the bid and far from ACT's latest $18.19 offer.

It plans to vote against Dacus and other nomination committee members at the next annual general meeting, citing poor governance and failed deals.

data.title: South Korean Stocks Close Higher
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI rose 0.3% to close at 2,570 points on Monday, reversing losses from the previous session, driven by auto and finance stocks.

The index also followed Wall Street’s Friday gains as global markets steadied despite ongoing US economic and tariff concerns.

In corporate news, Korea Zinc, the world's largest zinc refiner, surged 14.2%, its biggest gain since December, after a Seoul court nullified key board appointments, reigniting a takeover battle.

Among automakers, Hyundai Motor and Kia Corp advanced 0.4% and 1.2%, respectively.

In the financial sector, KB Financial (0.9%), Shinhan Financial (1.3%), and Hana Financial (2.4%) led the gains.

data.title: Danish Inflation Rises to 9-Month High
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Denmark rose to 2% in February 2025, the highest in nine months, from 1.5% in January.

Main upward pressure came from prices of food & non-alcoholic beverages (5.7% vs 4.4% in January) and housing & utilities (2.7% vs 1.9%).

Prices also increased for communication (3% vs 0.1%) and health (1.8% vs 1.4%), while rebounded for recreation & culture (1.1% vs -0.2%) and clothing & footwear (0.8%vs -0.8%).

On the other hand, price growth slowed for transport (0.3% vs 1%), alcoholic beverages & tobacco (1.2% vs 1.9%), restaurants & hotels (1.1% vs 1.9%), and miscellaneous goods & services (2% vs 2.2%).

Meanwhile, the core inflation, which excludes energy and unprocessed food, rose to a thirteen-month high of 1.8% in February, from 1.4% in the previous month.

On a monthly basis, consumer prices increased by 1%, following a 0.6% rise in January.

data.title: Germany's Monthly Industrial Output Up 2% in January
data.description:
Germany's industrial production gained 2% month over month in January, following a revised 1.5% fall in the previous month, provisional data from the Federal Statistical Office showed Monday.

Analysts expected a 1.5% rise for the month.

On a yearly basis, German industrial output was 1.6% lower, against the revised 2.2% decline earlier.

data.title: German Exports Fall for 1st Time in 3 Months
data.description:
Exports from Germany dropped by 2.5% month-over-month to EUR 129.2 billion in January 2025, reversing a downwardly revised 2.5% growth in December 2024.

This marked the first contraction in outbound shipments since last October, weighed down by sales to EU countries (-4.2%), specifically to the Euro area (-5.0%) and the non-Euro area (-2.3%).

Additionally, sales to third countries shrank by 0.4%, due to lower sales to the US (-4.2%) and China (-0.9%), but rising sales to the UK (1.7%) and Russia (7.2%).

In 2024, exports dropped by 1.0% to EUR 1.6 trillion.

data.title: New Talisman Gold Mines To Issue 1.7 Million Shares in Partial Conversion of Convertible Note
data.description:
New Talisman Gold Mines said it will issue 1.7 million ordinary shares in a partial conversion of the convertible note debt security issued to Hamish Brown, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

The amount payable for the shares will be NZ$69,300 at NZ$0.042 per share.

A full conversion of the note to ordinary shares would contravene the threshold of 20% of its total issued share capital under the New Zealand Takeovers Code.

The balance of the note will continue on existing terms for the remainder of the term under the board's discretion.

Further conversion of the note to shares may occur during that period if possible within the current regulatory regime.

data.title: German Trade Surplus Below Estimates
data.description:
Germany's trade surplus fell to EUR 16 billion in January 2025 from EUR 20.7 billion in December 2024, below forecasts of EUR 21 billion, marking the smallest trade surplus since last October due to a fall in exports.

Exports dropped by 2.5% month-on-month (mom) to EUR 129.2 billion in January, marking the first contraction in exports in three months.

Meanwhile, imports grew by 1.2% to a 16-month high of EUR 113.1 billion.

data.title: Diversified Energy, FuelCell Energy, Tesiac Agree to Form an Acquisition and Development Company
Watches of Switzerland to Launch $32 Million Buyback
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

Watches of Switzerland said it is launching a share buyback of up to 25 million pounds ($32.3 million).

The U.K. watch retailer said Monday that it is looking at a number of investment opportunities, both organic and inorganic, that it will prioritize, but that it has financial resources to pursue those while returning surplus capital to shareholders.

The buyback will start immediately, Watches of Switzerland said.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: Norva24 Group Receives SEK6.6 Billion Bid from Apax Funds
data.description:
Norva24 Group agreed to a recommended cash offer from Apax Funds through Nordahl BidCo valuing the former at 6.63 billion Swedish kronor.

Nordahl BidCo offered 36.50 kronor per share in cash for each Norva24 share, reflecting a 58.7% premium to the Nordic underground infrastructure maintenance company's closing price on March 7, according to a Monday release.

Norva24 noted that 57% of its shareholders have agreed to the offer, with the acceptance period open from March 28 until May 6. Closing of the offer is subject to requirements and regulatory approval.

Apax Funds also said it will launch a compulsory purchase and a delisting process if it secures more than a 90% stake in Norva24.
